AP Management.desc: Comprehensive AP configuration (alias of `show ap name <ap> config general`).
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# show ap config general AP-OFFICE-01
Cisco AP Name : AP-OFFICE-01
Cisco AP Identifier : cc6e.2aae.dc20
MAC Address : 70bd.9685.9c80
IP Address : 10.16.2.11
AP Mode : FlexConnect
Operation State : Registered
Number of Clients : 12

AP Management.desc: Reboot an AP. Tears down the CAPWAP tunnel; clients deauth and roam to neighbours (~30 sec downtime on that AP).
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-OFFICE-01 reset
WLC-01# show ap summary | include AP-OFFICE-01
AP-OFFICE-01  3   CW9166I-ROW  70bd.9685.9c80  cc6e.2aae.dc20  10.16.2.11  Joining
# After ~30 seconds:
AP-OFFICE-01  3   CW9166I-ROW  70bd.9685.9c80  cc6e.2aae.dc20  10.16.2.11  Registered

AP Management.cmd: show ap name AP-LON-01 config general
AP Management.desc: Full configuration for a specific AP (model, mode, tags).
AP Management.example:
AP Name    : AP-LON-01
AP Model   : C9130AXI-E
AP Mode    : Local
Policy Tag : CORP-POLICY
Site Tag   : LONDON-SITE

AP Management.desc: Rename a joined AP. The AP will rejoin the controller under its new name; client sessions on its radios will drop briefly during the rejoin.
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-TEMP-01 name AP-LON-01
WLC-01# show ap summary | include AP-LON-01
AP-LON-01  3  CW9166I-ROW  70bd.9685.9c80  cc6e.2aae.dc20  10.16.2.11  Registered

AP Management.cmd: ap name AP-LON-01 location "London HQ – 3rd Floor – East Wing"
AP Management.desc: Set a free-text location string on an AP. Used by RRM neighbour grouping and shown in WLC reports / Cisco DNA Center.
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 location "London HQ – 3rd Floor – East Wing"
WLC-01# show ap name AP-LON-01 config general | include Location
  AP Location                            : London HQ – 3rd Floor – East Wing
# Cleared with:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 location ""

AP Management.desc: Reboot a single AP via the controller. The AP CAPWAP tunnel drops, the AP reboots, then rejoins under the same tags.
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 reset
WLC-01# show ap summary | include AP-LON-01
AP-LON-01  3  CW9166I-ROW  70bd.9685.9c80  cc6e.2aae.dc20  10.16.2.11  Joining
# After ~30 sec:
AP-LON-01  3  CW9166I-ROW  70bd.9685.9c80  cc6e.2aae.dc20  10.16.2.11  Registered

AP Management.desc: Switch an AP to Local mode (default — centrally-switched data plane via the WLC). The AP reboots and rejoins.
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 mode local
Changing the AP's mode will cause the AP to reboot. Continue? [y/n]: y
WLC-01# show ap name AP-LON-01 config general | include Mode
  AP Mode                                : Local
  Operation State                         : Registered

AP Management.desc: Switch an AP to FlexConnect mode (locally-switched at the AP — survives WAN outages to the WLC). The AP reboots and rejoins.
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 mode flexconnect
Changing the AP's mode will cause the AP to reboot. Continue? [y/n]: y
WLC-01# show ap name AP-LON-01 config general | include Mode
  AP Mode                                : FlexConnect
  Operation State                         : Registered
# Verify flex profile is bound via site tag:
WLC-01# show ap tag summary | include AP-LON-01

AP Management.desc: Switch an AP to Monitor mode (no client serving — full-time spectrum / wIPS scan). The AP reboots and rejoins.
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 mode monitor
Changing the AP's mode will cause the AP to reboot. Continue? [y/n]: y

AP Management.desc: Turn off the AP status LED on AP-LON-01. Useful in lecture theatres, hotel rooms, conference rooms — anywhere the LED is distracting.
AP Management.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 led disable
WLC-01# show ap name AP-LON-01 config general | include LED
  AP LED State                        : Disabled
# Re-enable:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 led enable

AP Management.desc: Displays full detail for a specific AP including model, serial, join time, client count, and radio slot information
AP Management.example:
Cisco Controller> show wireless ap name AP-Lobby-01 detail
AP Name : AP-Lobby-01
AP Model: C9120AXI-B
AP Serial: FCW2344L0AB
IP Address: 10.1.2.50
Join Date : 06/20/2026 09:14:32
Clients (2.4G/5G/6G): 3/12/0
Slot 0 Radio: 2.4GHz  Channel: 6  Tx Power: 14 dBm
Slot 1 Radio: 5GHz    Channel: 36 Tx Power: 23 dBm

WLAN Configuration.desc: Create a WLAN. Profile name + ID + SSID. Profile name and SSID can differ. After creation, enter sub-mode to configure security.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wlan CORP-WLAN 1 CORPORATE-WIFI
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no security wpa wpa1
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa wpa2 ciphers aes
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm dot1x
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security pmf optional
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security dot1x authentication-list DOT1X-LIST
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no shutdown

WLAN Configuration.cmd: wlan <name> <id> <ssid> security wpa wpa2 ciphers aes
WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable WPA2 with AES (CCMP). Default for legacy/WPA2-only WLANs. Don't use TKIP — vulnerable.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wlan CORP-WLAN 1 CORPORATE-WIFI
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no security wpa wpa1
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa wpa2 ciphers aes
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include WPA|Encryption
  WPA (SSN IE)              : Disabled
  WPA2 (RSN IE)             : Enabled
      Encryption type       : CCMP-128

WLAN Configuration.cmd: wlan <name> <id> <ssid> security wpa psk set-key ascii 0 <passphrase>
WLAN Configuration.desc: Set PSK passphrase (ASCII 8-63 chars). `0` = enter unencrypted (stored encrypted in config). Use `0` for new keys, `7` for already-encrypted strings.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa psk set-key ascii 0 MyP@ssw0rd!
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include PSK
      Pre-shared Key      : Configured
      Pre-shared Key Format: ASCII

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable 802.1X EAP authentication. Requires a RADIUS server reachable for EAP exchanges.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm dot1x
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security dot1x authentication-list DOT1X-LIST
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include 802.1x|AAA
      Authentication Suites    : 802.1X
      AAA Authentication List  : DOT1X-LIST

WLAN Configuration.desc: Admin-disable a WLAN by ID. Existing clients are deauthed.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wlan 1 shutdown
WLC-01# show wlan summary | include ^1
1  CORP-WLAN  CORPORATE-WIFI  DOWN  [WPA2][802.1x]

WLAN Configuration.desc: Administratively enables a WLAN by ID
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# no wlan 1 shutdown
WLC-01# show wlan summary | include ^1
1   CORP-WLAN              CORPORATE-WIFI  UP  [WPA2][802.1x]

WLAN Configuration.desc: Broadcast the SSID in beacons (default). Hiding (no broadcast) provides minimal security benefit — clients still find it.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# broadcast-ssid
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include Broadcast
Broadcast SSID                        : Enabled

WLAN Configuration.desc: Hide the SSID — beacons sent without SSID name. Modern clients still find it via probe-request matching; security benefit is minimal.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no broadcast-ssid
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include Broadcast
Broadcast SSID                        : Disabled

WLAN Configuration.desc: Deletes a WLAN configuration by its numeric ID
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wlan 99 shutdown
WLC-01(config)# no wlan 99
WLC-01# show wlan summary | include 99
# (no output - WLAN deleted)

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable WPA3-SAE (replaces WPA2-PSK with Simultaneous Authentication of Equals).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa wpa3
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm sae
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa sae password 0 MyStrongPassword!
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security pmf required   ; mandatory for WPA3

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable band-select — defer 2.4 GHz association so dual-band clients prefer 5/6 GHz.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# band-select
WLC-01# show wireless client steering stats | include defer
  Number of clients deferred: 234

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable Cisco Compatible Extensions (CCX) Aironet IE support — legacy feature for older Cisco wireless clients. Usually disabled in modern deployments.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# ccx aironet-iesupport
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include CCX|Aironet
  CCX - AironetIeSupport: Enabled
  CCX - Gratuitous ProbeResponse (GPR): Disabled

WLAN Configuration.desc: DTIM (Delivery Traffic Indication Map) period in beacons. Higher = better battery life (clients sleep longer) but more multicast buffering delay. Default 1. Voice WLANs: keep at 1; data-only WLANs: 2-3.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# dtim dot11 24ghz 2
WLC-01(config-wlan)# dtim dot11 5ghz 2
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include DTIM
  DTIM Period for 2.4 GHz                : 2
  DTIM Period for 5 GHz                  : 2

WLAN Configuration.desc: Generic WLAN create syntax — pick a profile name, numeric ID (1-512), and SSID broadcast name. After creation, enter config-wlan mode.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wlan GUEST-WLAN 2 GUEST-WIFI
WLC-01(config-wlan)# description "Guest WiFi captive portal"
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no security wpa
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no shutdown

WLAN Configuration.desc: Within wlan config sub-mode: set the SSID. Only needed if you change SSID after creation.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# ssid CORPORATE-WIFI
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include SSID
Network Name (SSID)         : CORPORATE-WIFI

WLAN Configuration.desc: Free-text description on a WLAN, policy profile, AP profile, RF profile, or site tag. Pure documentation — visible in show output.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# description "Corporate WiFi with 802.1X EAP-TLS"
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include Description
Description                : Corporate WiFi with 802.1X EAP-TLS

WLAN Configuration.desc: Within a policy tag: bind a policy profile to a WLAN. This is the linkage that ties WLAN config (SSID, security) to a policy profile (VLAN, AAA override, switching mode).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless tag policy POLICY-TAG-FL1
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# wlan CORP-WLAN policy POLICY-FL1
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# end
WLC-01# show wireless tag policy detailed POLICY-TAG-FL1
Number of WLAN-POLICY maps: 1
WLAN Profile Name      Policy Name
CORP-WLAN              POLICY-FL1

WLAN Configuration.desc: Remove a WLAN→policy-profile binding from a policy tag.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# no wlan CORP-WLAN policy POLICY-FL1
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# end
WLC-01# show wireless tag policy detailed POLICY-TAG-FL1
Number of WLAN-POLICY maps: 0

WLAN Configuration.desc: Set PSK passphrase (8-63 ASCII chars). Use 0 for cleartext input, 7 for already-encrypted.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa psk set-key ascii 0 MyP@ssw0rd!
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show running-config | section wlan GUEST-WLAN
wlan GUEST-WLAN 2 GUEST-WIFI
 security wpa psk set-key ascii 7 <encrypted-blob>

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable WPA pre-shared-key AKM. Pair with `security wpa psk set-key ...`.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa wpa2
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm psk
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa psk set-key ascii 0 MyP@ssw0rd!

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable WPA 802.1X (EAP) AKM. Pair with `security dot1x authentication-list <list>`.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm dot1x
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security dot1x authentication-list DOT1X-LIST
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include AKM
      Auth Key Management         : 802.1x

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind a named AAA dot1x method list to a WLAN. The list must be defined globally with `aaa authentication dot1x <list> group <radius-group>`.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security dot1x authentication-list DOT1X-LIST
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include AAA
  AAA Authentication List         : DOT1X-LIST
  AAA Authorization List          : DOT1X-LIST

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable WPA3 SAE (Simultaneous Authentication of Equals) AKM — replaces WPA2-PSK with a stronger key-exchange. Requires PMF mandatory.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa wpa3
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm sae
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa sae password 0 MyStrongPassword!
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security pmf required
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include WPA3|SAE|PMF
  WPA3 (WPA3 IE)            : Enabled
      Auth Key Management   : SAE
  PMF                        : Required

WLAN Configuration.desc: SAE password (WPA3 equivalent of PSK). 8-63 ASCII chars.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa sae password 0 MyStrongPassword!

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable WPA3 (can coexist with WPA2 for transition mode, or alone for WPA3-only).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa wpa3
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa wpa2   ; optional - transition mode
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include WPA
  WPA2 (RSN IE)             : Enabled
  WPA3 (WPA3 IE)            : Enabled

WLAN Configuration.desc: WPA3-Enterprise Suite-B (192-bit) — government-grade. Requires ECDSA-P384 server cert. Most deployments use 802.1X SHA256 instead.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm suite-b-192
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include AKM|GCMP
      Auth Key Management         : 802.1x-SUITEB-192
      Encryption type             : GCMP-256

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disable WPA entirely — open WLAN. Use only for guest captive-portal flows where Layer 3 web auth replaces Layer 2 security.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no security wpa
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 2 | include WPA|802.11
  802.11 Authentication    : Open System
  WPA (SSN IE)             : Disabled
  WPA2 (RSN IE)            : Disabled

WLAN Configuration.desc: Remove all WPA AKMs from a WLAN. Often used as a reset before configuring a new AKM.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no security wpa akm
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm sae   ; reconfigure with SAE only

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disable 802.1X auth on the WLAN.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no security dot1x
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 2 | include 802.1x
  Authentication Suites    : <none>

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable MAC filtering using a named AAA list (used in MAB / hybrid auth flows where MAC address is queried as the username).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# mac-filtering DOT1X-LIST
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include MAC
  MAC Filtering              : DOT1X-LIST

WLAN Configuration.desc: Create/enter policy profile sub-mode. Policy profile defines per-WLAN switching, VLAN, AAA override, central/local mode.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless profile policy POLICY-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# description "Floor 1 corporate policy"
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# vlan CORP-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# aaa-override
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no central switching   ; FlexConnect
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no shutdown

WLAN Configuration.desc: Within policy profile: bind a VLAN to clients. ID for centrally-switched; VLAN name for FlexConnect (must match a vlan-name defined in the flex profile).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# vlan 1110               ; centrally-switched, numeric
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# vlan CORP-FL1           ; FlexConnect, by name
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include VLAN
VLAN                  : CORP-FL1

WLAN Configuration.desc: Remove VLAN binding from policy profile (clients fall back to native).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no vlan 1110

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable a WLAN / policy profile / flex profile. Must be enabled before clients can associate.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no shutdown
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan summary | include CORP-WLAN
1  CORP-WLAN  CORPORATE-WIFI  UP  [WPA2][802.1x]

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disable a WLAN / policy profile / flex profile. Existing clients are deauthed.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# shutdown
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan summary | include CORP-WLAN
1  CORP-WLAN  CORPORATE-WIFI  DOWN  [WPA2][802.1x]

WLAN Configuration.desc: Maximum client session length before forced re-auth. Default 1800 (30 min). Common: 7200 (2 hours) for corporate.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# session-timeout 7200
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include Session
Session Timeout (sec)    : 7200

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disconnect a client after N seconds of no traffic. Default 300 (5 min). Reduce for shared IoT VLANs to free DHCP leases.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# idle-timeout 600
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include Idle
Idle Timeout (sec)        : 600

WLAN Configuration.desc: Attach a QoS policy-map to ingress (client → WLC) traffic.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# service-policy input QOS-INGRESS

WLAN Configuration.desc: Attach a QoS policy-map to egress (WLC → client) traffic.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# service-policy output QOS-EGRESS

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enter wlan sub-mode by name (for an already-created WLAN). Use to edit an existing WLAN.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wlan CORP-WLAN
WLC-01(config-wlan)# session-timeout 7200

WLAN Configuration.desc: Allow AAA server (RADIUS) returned attributes to override policy-profile defaults (VLAN, ACL, session timeout). Essential when using ISE for dynamic VLAN/policy assignment.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# aaa-override
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include AAA
AAA Policy
    AAA Override           : ENABLED

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind a RADIUS accounting list to a policy profile. Sends start/stop/interim records to the accounting server.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# accounting-list DOT1X-LIST

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind a RADIUS authentication list to a policy profile.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wlan)# security dot1x authentication-list DOT1X-LIST

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind a RADIUS authorization list (returns VLAN/ACL via VSAs).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# authorization-list DOT1X-LIST

WLAN Configuration.desc: In policy profile: enable local (FlexConnect) switching — traffic stays at the branch, never tunnels back to the WLC. Synonym of `no central switching`.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no central switching
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include Central
Central Switching          : DISABLED

WLAN Configuration.desc: In policy profile: enable central switching — all client traffic tunnels back to the WLC (default for non-Flex deployments).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# central switching
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include Central
Central Switching          : ENABLED

WLAN Configuration.desc: In policy profile: enable local 802.1X authentication at the AP (FlexConnect local-auth — no WAN round-trip per auth). AP itself talks RADIUS direct.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no central authentication
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include Central
Central Authentication     : DISABLED

WLAN Configuration.desc: In policy profile: central authentication via WLC (default).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# central authentication

WLAN Configuration.desc: Set FlexConnect VLAN on a policy profile. Equivalent to `vlan <id>` in modern syntax.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# vlan 1110

WLAN Configuration.desc: Per-AP FlexConnect VLAN override — bound at site-tag/flex-profile level on modern 9800.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless tag site SITE-FL1
WLC-01(config-site-tag)# flex-profile FLEX-FL1

WLAN Configuration.desc: Anchor a WLAN to a remote WLC (mobility group peer). Common for guest WiFi — guests tunnel to a DMZ WLC for isolation.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# mobility anchor 10.99.99.10
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless mobility anchor
WLAN ID: 2 (GUEST-WLAN)
  Anchor WLC: 10.99.99.10 (DMZ-WLC)
  Status: Active
  Anchored Clients: 23

WLAN Configuration.desc: Remove a mobility anchor binding.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no mobility anchor 10.99.99.10

WLAN Configuration.desc: Mobility tunnel mode for inter-WLC roams — L2 (transparent bridge) or L3 (anchor tunnel).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# mobility tunnel mode l3

WLAN Configuration.desc: Per-client session timeout (alias of `session-timeout`).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# session-timeout 7200

WLAN Configuration.desc: Per-client idle timeout (alias of `idle-timeout`).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# idle-timeout 600

WLAN Configuration.desc: Exclusion-list timeout — how long a misbehaving client is locked out. Default 60 sec.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# exclusion-list timeout 300

WLAN Configuration.desc: Inter-controller roaming timeout. Default 4 sec — clients must complete the roam within this window or the session is dropped.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# roaming-timeout 4

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind a QoS profile to a WLAN. Modern 9800 uses MQC: `service-policy input/output <policy-map>`.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# service-policy input QOS-INGRESS
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# service-policy output QOS-EGRESS

WLAN Configuration.desc: Remove a QoS profile binding.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no service-policy input QOS-INGRESS

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind an Application Visibility & Control profile (DPI-based app detection + optional drop policies for streaming, social media).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# ip flow monitor AVC-MON input
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# ip flow monitor AVC-MON output
WLC-01# show wireless avc summary
AVC Enabled: Yes
Top App: Microsoft Teams

WLAN Configuration.desc: Remove AVC profile binding.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no ip flow monitor AVC-MON input

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind a NetFlow monitor to the policy profile for per-client flow export to a collector (e.g. Stealthwatch, ntopng).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# ip flow monitor MON1 input
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show flow monitor MON1 cache | head -5
Cache Statistics: 12345 active flows

WLAN Configuration.desc: Multicast VLAN — overrides where downstream multicast is sourced from. Used to dedicate a single VLAN for video/IPTV multicast feeds.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# multicast vlan 200

WLAN Configuration.desc: Convert downstream multicast to per-client unicast. Improves video reliability at the cost of airtime (each client gets its own copy).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# multicast mcast-to-unicast
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include Multicast
  Multicast to Unicast Conversion : Enabled

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disable multicast conversion.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no multicast mcast-to-unicast

WLAN Configuration.desc: Within wlan sub-mode: broadcast the SSID in beacons (default).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wlan)# broadcast-ssid

WLAN Configuration.desc: Hide SSID — beacons sent without SSID name. Modern clients still find it. Provides minimal security benefit.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wlan)# no broadcast-ssid

WLAN Configuration.desc: PMF (Protected Management Frames, 802.11w) mandatory. Required for WPA3. Blocks deauth/disassoc spoofing attacks.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security pmf required
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include PMF
  PMF                       : Required

WLAN Configuration.desc: PMF negotiated when client supports it. Required for WPA2/WPA3 transition mode (some legacy clients can't do PMF).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security pmf optional
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include PMF
  PMF                       : Optional

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disable PMF entirely (legacy WPA2 only, not allowed with WPA3).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wlan)# security pmf disable

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable 6 GHz radio operation on this WLAN. Required for Wi-Fi 6E.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# dot11 6ghz
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include 6 GHz
  6 GHz Network                          : Enabled

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disables 6 GHz radio band support on the WLAN profile — use when client devices don't support the WPA3/OWE-only requirements of 6E, or when no 6 GHz radios are provisioned on the AP group bound to this WLAN.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no dot11 6ghz
WLC-01(config-wlan)# do show wlan id 5 | include 6 GHz
802.11 6GHz                                     : Disabled
# 6 GHz mandates WPA3/OWE security per the Wi-Fi Alliance 6E spec — disabling
# it here does not relax that security requirement on the 2.4/5 GHz radios.

WLAN Configuration.desc: AP-profile regulatory domain (auto-derived from country code; rarely set explicitly).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-ap-profile)# regulatory-domain GB

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind 2.4 GHz RF profile to an RF tag.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-rf-tag)# 24ghz-rf-policy Typical_Client_Density_rf_24gh

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind 5 GHz RF profile to an RF tag.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-rf-tag)# 5ghz-rf-policy Typical_Client_Density_rf_5gh

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind 6 GHz RF profile to an RF tag.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-rf-tag)# 6ghz-rf-policy default-rf-profile-6ghz

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind an mDNS service-discovery profile to a policy profile — enables cross-VLAN AirPlay / Bonjour / Chromecast discovery.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# mdns-sd-policy MDNS-DEFAULT

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable band-select on the WLAN — defer 2.4 GHz association responses so dual-band clients prefer 5/6 GHz.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# band-select
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wireless client steering stats | include defer
  Number of clients deferred: 234

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disables band steering (band select) on the WLAN, allowing dual-band clients to associate to either 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz without being nudged toward 5 GHz — useful when 2.4 GHz reach is needed.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no band-select
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end

# Verify:
WLC-01# show wireless wlan summary
WLAN ID  Profile Name    SSID             Band Select
1        Corp-WiFi       Corporate_SSID   Disabled

# Re-enable band steering:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# band-select

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable AP load-balancing on the WLAN. Clients are steered toward less-loaded APs.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# load-balance
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include Load
  Load Balancing                : Enabled

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disables load balancing on the WLAN, preventing the WLC from redirecting clients to less-loaded APs — use when sticky clients are causing connectivity complaints after the redirect.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# no load-balance
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end

# Verify:
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include load.balance
  Load Balance                         : Disabled

# Re-enable:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# load-balance

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable client steering (band-select + AP-suggested roaming).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# steering-client

WLAN Configuration.desc: Disables 802.11v BSS Transition-based client steering on the WLAN, stopping the WLC from sending transition requests to clients — useful when clients mishandle the BSS-TM request.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no steering-client
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end

# Verify:
WLC-01# show wireless client steering
Client Steering Status : Disabled

# Note: disabling steering may increase cell edge congestion on busy deployments

WLAN Configuration.desc: Detailed WLAN configuration for the specified WLAN ID.
WLAN Configuration.example:
SSID     : Corp
VLAN     : 100
Security : WPA2/WPA3

WLAN Configuration.desc: 802.11r Fast Transition in adaptive mode (capable clients use FT; legacy clients fall back to full re-auth). Default for WPA3 transition deployments.
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security ft adaptive
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include FT
  FT                          : Adaptive

WLAN Configuration.desc: 802.11r Fast Transition over-the-DS variant — pre-auth via the distribution system (wired) before the client roams to the new AP.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wlan)# security ft over-the-ds

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable 802.1X SHA-256 AKM (required for PMF / WPA3 transition mode).
WLAN Configuration.example:
WLC-01(config-wlan)# security wpa akm dot1x-sha256
WLC-01(config-wlan)# end
WLC-01# show wlan id 1 | include 802.1x-SHA
      Auth Key Management         : 802.1X SHA256

WLAN Configuration.desc: Bind a named AAA dot1x method list to the WLAN.
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wlan)# security dot1x authentication-list DOT1X-LIST

WLAN Configuration.desc: Enable MAC filtering using a named AAA list (MAB / hybrid auth — MAC sent as username to RADIUS).
WLAN Configuration.example: WLC-01(config-wlan)# mac-filtering DOT1X-LIST

Tags & Profiles.desc: Create/enter a site tag — binds Flex profile and AP join profile to a group of APs.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless tag site SITE-FL1
WLC-01(config-site-tag)# description "Floor 1 Site Tag"
WLC-01(config-site-tag)# flex-profile FLEX-FL1
WLC-01(config-site-tag)# ap-profile default-ap-profile

Tags & Profiles.desc: Within a site tag: bind an AP join profile. AP join profile controls management creds, CAPWAP timers, SSH/Telnet.
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-site-tag)# ap-profile default-ap-profile

Tags & Profiles.desc: Create/enter a policy tag — maps WLANs to policy profiles for a group of APs.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless tag policy POLICY-TAG-FL1
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# wlan CORP-WLAN policy POLICY-FL1
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# wlan GUEST-WLAN policy GUEST-POLICY

Tags & Profiles.desc: Maps a WLAN to a wireless policy profile within a policy tag
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# wlan CORP-WLAN policy POLICY-FL1
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# wlan GUEST-WLAN policy GUEST-POLICY
WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# end
WLC-01# show wireless tag policy detailed POLICY-TAG-FL1
Number of WLAN-POLICY maps: 2
WLAN Profile Name          Policy Name
CORP-WLAN                  POLICY-FL1
GUEST-WLAN                 GUEST-POLICY

Tags & Profiles.desc: Create/enter an RF tag — binds per-band RF profiles to a group of APs.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless tag rf HIGH-DENSITY
WLC-01(config-wireless-rf-tag)# 24ghz-rf-policy HIGH-DENSITY-24
WLC-01(config-wireless-rf-tag)# 5ghz-rf-policy HIGH-DENSITY-5

Tags & Profiles.desc: Associates a 2.4GHz RF policy with this RF tag
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-rf-tag)# 24ghz-rf-policy Typical_Client_Density_rf_24gh

Tags & Profiles.desc: Associates a 5GHz RF policy with this RF tag
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-rf-tag)# 5ghz-rf-policy Typical_Client_Density_rf_5gh

Tags & Profiles.desc: Create/enter a policy profile — defines per-WLAN switching, VLAN, AAA override.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless profile policy POLICY-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# vlan CORP-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# aaa-override
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no central switching   ; for FlexConnect
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no shutdown

Tags & Profiles.desc: Associates a data VLAN with the wireless policy profile
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# vlan 1110

Tags & Profiles.desc: Create/enter an AP join profile (legacy syntax — modern is `ap profile <name>`).
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# ap profile default-ap-profile
WLC-01(config-ap-profile)# mgmtuser username admin password 0 <login-pass> secret 0 <enable-secret>

Tags & Profiles.cmd: ap name AP-LON-01 tag site LONDON-SITE
Tags & Profiles.desc: Assign a site tag to an AP. Site tags carry AP-join profile, flex profile, and RRM grouping.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 tag site LONDON-SITE
WLC-01# show ap tag summary | include AP-LON-01
AP-LON-01  70bd.9685.9c80  LONDON-SITE  POLICY-FL1  HIGH-DENSITY  No  Static

Tags & Profiles.cmd: ap name AP-LON-01 tag policy CORP-POLICY
Tags & Profiles.desc: Assign a policy tag to an AP. Policy tags map WLAN profiles → policy profiles for this AP.
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 tag policy CORP-POLICY

Tags & Profiles.cmd: ap name AP-LON-01 tag rf HIGH-DENSITY
Tags & Profiles.desc: Assign an RF tag to an AP. RF tags hold per-band RF profiles (DCA / TPC / channel widths / data-rate sets).
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 tag rf HIGH-DENSITY

Tags & Profiles.desc: FlexConnect profile defining VLAN-name to VLAN-ID mappings for locally-switched WLANs, plus the native VLAN. Reference VLANs by name in the policy profile.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless profile flex FLEX-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-profile)# description "Floor 1 Flex Profile"
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-profile)# native-vlan-id 10
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-profile)# vlan-name CORP-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-vlan)#  vlan-id 1110
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-profile)# vlan-name AUTH-FAILED
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-vlan)#  vlan-id 1168
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-vlan)#   central-dhcp

Tags & Profiles.desc: Flex profile: defines the native VLAN of the AP uplink trunk.
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-profile)# native-vlan-id 10

Tags & Profiles.desc: Flex profile: declare a VLAN name that the policy profile can reference. Pair with `vlan-id <id>` underneath.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-profile)# vlan-name CORP-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-vlan)#  vlan-id 1110

Tags & Profiles.desc: Force DHCP for a specific Flex VLAN back through CAPWAP. Used for the auth-failed quarantine VLAN so the WLC can serve it locally.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-profile)# vlan-name AUTH-FAILED
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-vlan)#  vlan-id 1168
WLC-01(config-wireless-flex-vlan)#   central-dhcp

Tags & Profiles.desc: Site tag: binds a flex profile to the APs that use this site tag.
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-site-tag)# flex-profile FLEX-FL1

Tags & Profiles.desc: Site tag: binds an AP join profile to the APs that use this site tag.
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-site-tag)# ap-profile default-ap-profile

Tags & Profiles.desc: Site tag: enable FlexConnect (default — local switching at the site). Setting `no local-site` puts APs into central switching, which is the single most common FlexConnect misconfiguration.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config-site-tag)# local-site
WLC-01(config-site-tag)# end
WLC-01# show wireless tag site detailed SITE-FL1 | include Local
Local-site         : Yes

Tags & Profiles.desc: Policy tag: maps a WLAN to a policy profile.
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-policy-tag)# wlan CORP-WLAN policy POLICY-FL1

Tags & Profiles.desc: Create/enter an AP join profile. Controls management credentials, CAPWAP timers, SSH/Telnet access. Bound to APs via site tag.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# ap profile default-ap-profile
WLC-01(config-ap-profile)# mgmtuser username admin password 0 <login> secret 0 <enable>

Tags & Profiles.desc: AP join profile: management credentials. Both login password AND enable secret must be set on the same line — they're separate authentications used when SSH'ing to the AP.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config-ap-profile)# mgmtuser username admin password 0 <login-pass> secret 0 <enable-secret>
WLC-01(config-ap-profile)# end
WLC-01# show ap profile name default-ap-profile detailed | include mgmt|username
Management User: admin

Tags & Profiles.desc: Per-AP tag override: enter per-AP config mode to assign policy/site/RF tags to a single AP.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# ap aabb.ccdd.eeff
WLC-01(config-ap-tag)# policy-tag POLICY-TAG-FL1
WLC-01(config-ap-tag)# site-tag   SITE-FL1
WLC-01(config-ap-tag)# rf-tag     RF-DEFAULT

Tags & Profiles.cmd: ap filter create <name> / ap name-regex <regex> / ap filter priority <n> filter-name <name>
Tags & Profiles.desc: Regex-based bulk tag assignment by AP name.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# ap filter create FILTER-FL1
WLC-01(config-ap-filter)# ap name-regex ^AP-FL1-.*
WLC-01(config)# ap filter priority 1 filter-name FILTER-FL1

Tags & Profiles.desc: Policy profile for FlexConnect. Defaults to shutdown and central switching. For FlexConnect: leave central authentication and central association at default (enabled); only central switching and central dhcp are disabled.
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless profile policy POLICY-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# description "Floor 1 Corp Policy"
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# vlan CORP-FL1
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# aaa-override
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# nac
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no central switching
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no central dhcp
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no shutdown

Tags & Profiles.desc: Policy profile: disable central switching (required for FlexConnect local switching).
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no central switching
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include Central Switching
Central Switching          : DISABLED

Tags & Profiles.desc: Policy profile: disable central DHCP (FlexConnect — DHCP handled locally at the branch).
Tags & Profiles.example: WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# no central dhcp

Tags & Profiles.desc: Policy profile: enable Network Admission Control / RADIUS CoA integration with an NAC server (e.g. ISE posture).
Tags & Profiles.example:
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# nac
WLC-01(config-wireless-policy)# end
WLC-01# show wireless profile policy detailed POLICY-FL1 | include NAC
  NAC                      : ENABLED

Client Management.desc: Older-syntax client detail (use `show wireless client mac-address <mac> detail` going forward).
Client Management.example:
WLC-01# show wireless client detail mac-address aabb.ccdd.eeff
# Same output as `show wireless client mac-address aabb.ccdd.eeff detail`.

Client Management.desc: Forcibly deauth a client. Client immediately disassociates and must re-auth.
Client Management.example:
WLC-01# wireless client mac-address aabb.ccdd.eeff deauthenticate
Client aabb.ccdd.eeff deauthenticated

Client Management.desc: Traffic and session statistics for a specific client
Client Management.example:
WLC-01# show wireless client statistics mac-address aabb.ccdd.eeff
Client MAC Address : aabb.ccdd.eeff
Total Packets RX : 12345
Total Packets TX : 8765
Total Bytes RX   : 2345678
Total Bytes TX   : 1234567
Retries          : 12 (0.10%)
RSSI (dBm)       : -45
SNR (dB)          : 50

Client Management.desc: Lists all clients currently associated to a specific AP
Client Management.example:
WLC-01# show wireless client ap AP-OFFICE-01
MAC Address       AP    Slot State    Auth Method
1122.3344.5566    1     1    Run      Dot1x
2233.4455.6677    1     1    Run      Dot1x
3344.5566.7788    1     2    Run      PSK
Total: 3 clients

Client Management.desc: Lists all clients currently associated to a specific WLAN
Client Management.example:
WLC-01# show wireless client wlan CORP-WLAN
MAC Address       AP Name        State    Auth
1122.3344.5566    AP-OFFICE-01   Run      Dot1x
2233.4455.6677    AP-OFFICE-01   Run      Dot1x
4455.6677.8899    AP-OFFICE-02   Run      Dot1x
Total: 3 clients

Client Management.desc: Clients on the exclusion list (locked out due to misbehaviour or admin action).
Client Management.example:
WLC-01# show wireless exclusionlist
Client MAC          Reason                    AP             Time on List
aabb.ccdd.9999     802.1x Authentication Fail  AP-OFFICE-01   00:04:55
ccdd.eeff.1111     PSK Failure                 AP-OFFICE-02   00:01:23

Client Management.desc: Adds a specific client MAC to the exclusion (blacklist)
Client Management.example: WLC-01# wireless client mac-address aabb.ccdd.9999 exclude

Client Management.desc: Delete a client entry from the WLC database (forces fresh association state).
Client Management.example: WLC-01# clear wireless client mac-address aabb.ccdd.eeff delete

Client Management.desc: Displays band steering statistics: steered clients and steering failures
Client Management.example:
WLC-01# show wireless client steering stats
Client Steering Statistics:
  Total steering decisions     : 12345
  Band-select deferrals         :  4567
  6 GHz steers                  :  1234
  5 GHz steers                  :  8765
  Steering rejects (client)     :   234

Client Management.desc: Detailed client information for one MAC: IP, VLAN, AP, RSSI, SNR.
Client Management.example:
IP Address : 10.50.100.27
VLAN       : 100
AP Name    : AP-LON-01
RSSI       : -55 dBm
SNR        : 38 dB

RRM.desc: RF Resource Management — RF grouping (which WLCs share RRM domain).
RRM.example:
WLC-01# show wireless rrm rf-grouping
Group Name : default-group
  Member Controllers:
    1.1.1.1 (this) - Leader
    1.1.1.2 - Member
    1.1.1.3 - Member
  Last RRM grouping run: 14:00:00 Apr 21 2026

RRM.desc: Triggers an immediate RRM channel assignment update for all 2.4GHz radios
RRM.example:
WLC-01# ap dot11 24ghz rrm channel update
RRM channel update triggered. Apply within ~30 sec.

RRM.desc: Triggers an immediate RRM channel assignment update for all 5GHz radios
RRM.example: WLC-01# ap dot11 5ghz rrm channel update

RRM.desc: Force RRM TPC (Transmit Power Control) re-computation on 2.4 GHz.
RRM.example: WLC-01# ap dot11 24ghz rrm txpower update

RRM.desc: Triggers an immediate RRM transmit power update for 5GHz
RRM.example: WLC-01# ap dot11 5ghz rrm txpower update

RRM.desc: RRM detail for the 2.4 GHz band — last DCA run, channel reasoning, noise, interferers.
RRM.example:
WLC-01# show ap auto-rf dot11 24ghz
Number of APs                          : 48
Leader                                  : 1.1.1.1
Last Update                             : 12:00:00 Apr 21 2026
Next Update                             : 16:00:00 Apr 21 2026
DCA Mode                                : Auto
DCA Channel Width                        : 20 MHz
DCA Sensitivity                          : Medium
Channels in use: 1, 6, 11

RRM.desc: Auto-RF report for all 5GHz radios: channel, power, coverage overlap
RRM.example:
WLC-01# show ap auto-rf dot11 5ghz
DCA Mode                  : Auto
DCA Channel Width          : 40 MHz
DCA Sensitivity            : Medium
DFS Channels Enabled       : Yes
Channels in use: 36, 40, 44, 48, 149, 153, 157, 161

RRM.cmd: ap dot11 5ghz rrm channel dca remove <channel>
RRM.desc: Remove a channel from DCA consideration (e.g. exclude a DFS channel known to cause radar false-positives).
RRM.example:
WLC-01# ap dot11 5ghz rrm channel dca remove 100
Channel 100 removed from DCA selection.

RRM.desc: Enables logging of all RRM-initiated channel changes
RRM.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless rrm logging channel-change
WLC-01# show logging | include RRM-CHAN
%RRM-CHAN: AP-OFFICE-01 5GHz channel changed from 149 to 36 (reason: load-balance)

RRM.desc: Override the RRM-assigned 2.4 GHz channel on a single AP. RRM stops adjusting this radio until the override is cleared.
RRM.example: WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 dot11 24ghz channel 6

RRM.desc: Override the RRM-assigned 5 GHz channel on a single AP. RRM stops adjusting this radio until the override is cleared.
RRM.example: WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 dot11 5ghz channel 36

RRM.desc: Override the 2.4 GHz transmit-power level (1 = max, 8 = min). Disables RRM TPC for this radio.
RRM.example: WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 dot11 24ghz txpower 3

RRM.desc: Override the 5 GHz transmit-power level (1 = max, 8 = min). Disables RRM TPC for this radio.
RRM.example: WLC-01# ap name AP-LON-01 dot11 5ghz txpower 4

Security & AAA.desc: Enable the AAA subsystem. Required before any AAA-based feature (802.1X, TACACS+ login, RADIUS for VTY).
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# aaa new-model
WLC-01(config)# aaa session-id common

Security & AAA.cmd: aaa authentication login <list-name> group radius local
Security & AAA.desc: AAA authentication method list for login: try RADIUS group first, fall back to local. Always include a local fallback to avoid lockout if RADIUS is unreachable.
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# aaa authentication login VTY-LIST group RAD-GROUP local
WLC-01(config)# line vty 0 4
WLC-01(config-line)# login authentication VTY-LIST

Security & AAA.cmd: aaa authentication dot1x default group radius
Security & AAA.desc: Configures 802.1X authentication to use a RADIUS server group
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# aaa authentication dot1x default group RAD-GROUP

Security & AAA.desc: Define a RADIUS server entry. Pair with `address`, `key`, `automate-tester` sub-commands.
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# radius server NPS-01
WLC-01(config-radius-server)# address ipv4 10.10.10.10 auth-port 1812 acct-port 1813
WLC-01(config-radius-server)# timeout 5
WLC-01(config-radius-server)# retransmit 3
WLC-01(config-radius-server)# automate-tester username radius-probe ignore-acct-port probe-on
WLC-01(config-radius-server)# key <shared-secret>

Security & AAA.desc: RADIUS server address sub-command. Standard ports: 1812 auth, 1813 accounting. Legacy: 1645/1646.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config-radius-server)# address ipv4 10.10.10.10 auth-port 1812 acct-port 1813

Security & AAA.desc: RADIUS shared secret. Use `key 7 <encrypted>` for already-encrypted input.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config-radius-server)# key <shared-secret>

Security & AAA.desc: RADIUS server reachability status. ALIVE = healthy, DEAD = stopped responding.
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01# show aaa servers
RADIUS: id 1, priority 1, host 10.10.10.10, auth-port 1812, acct-port 1813
     State: current UP, duration 1209655s, previous duration 0s
     Platform State from SMD: current UP
     Authen: request 18452, timeouts 3, failover 0, retransmission 3
             Response: accept 17901, reject 548, challenge 0
             Transaction: success 18449, failure 3

Security & AAA.desc: Displays RADIUS transaction statistics and error counts
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01# show radius statistics
Auth.    : Maximum inQ length: 1
Auth.    : Total responses seen from server: 18449
Auth.    : Packets with responses: 18449
Auth.    : Packets without responses: 3
Auth.    : Average response time (msec): 12

Security & AAA.desc: Globally enable MAC filtering. Pair with per-WLAN `mac-filtering <aaa-list>`.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# wireless mac-filtering

Security & AAA.desc: Displays the MAC filter list and allowed/denied entries
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01# show wireless client mac-filter
MAC Address       Description           Auth Type
aabb.ccdd.eeff   Trusted device         Local
1122.3344.5566   Printer floor 3        Local

Security & AAA.desc: Use a common session-id format across AAA subsystems (recommended for ISE/NPS compatibility).
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# aaa session-id common

Security & AAA.desc: Periodic RADIUS probe so the WLC marks a dead server before a real client times out. The probe user doesn't need to exist — the WLC just needs any RADIUS reply.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config-radius-server)# automate-tester username radius-probe ignore-acct-port probe-on

Security & AAA.desc: RADIUS per-server response timeout (default 5 sec).
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config-radius-server)# timeout 5

Security & AAA.desc: RADIUS server: per-server retransmit attempts before failover.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config-radius-server)# retransmit 3

Security & AAA.desc: Group RADIUS servers for load-balancing and failover.
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# aaa group server radius RAD-GROUP
WLC-01(config-sg-radius)# server name NPS-01
WLC-01(config-sg-radius)# server name NPS-02
WLC-01(config-sg-radius)# deadtime 5
WLC-01(config-sg-radius)# load-balance method least-outstanding

Security & AAA.desc: How quickly the WLC marks a non-responding server dead — after N consecutive timeouts across at least <s> seconds.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# radius-server dead-criteria time 5 tries 3

Security & AAA.desc: How long a server stays marked dead before being probed again. Default 0 (immediate retry).
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# radius-server deadtime 5

Security & AAA.desc: Named 802.1X authentication method list pointing at a server group.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# aaa authentication dot1x DOT1X-LIST group RAD-GROUP

Security & AAA.desc: AAA method list for network authorization (returns VLAN/ACL attributes).
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# aaa authorization network DOT1X-LIST group RAD-GROUP

Security & AAA.desc: AAA accounting for identity (dot1x) sessions — sends start/stop/interim records.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# aaa accounting identity DOT1X-LIST start-stop group RAD-GROUP

Security & AAA.desc: Enable RADIUS Change of Authorization (CoA). Required for guest captive portals and post-auth VLAN re-assignment.
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# aaa server radius dynamic-author
WLC-01(config-locsvr-da-radius)# client 10.10.10.10 server-key <shared-secret>
WLC-01(config-locsvr-da-radius)# client 10.10.10.11 server-key <shared-secret>
WLC-01(config-locsvr-da-radius)# auth-type any

Security & AAA.desc: Tune which RADIUS attributes the WLC sends/expects. Common attrs: 6 (Service-Type), 8 (Framed-IP), 25 (Class), 31 (Calling-Station-ID).
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# radius-server attribute 6 on-for-login-auth
WLC-01(config)# radius-server attribute 8 include-in-access-req
WLC-01(config)# radius-server attribute 25 access-request include
WLC-01(config)# radius-server attribute 31 mac format ietf upper-case
WLC-01(config)# radius-server attribute 31 send nas-port-detail mac-only
WLC-01(config)# radius-server vsa send authentication
WLC-01(config)# radius-server vsa send accounting

Security & AAA.desc: Timezone setting. Required before NTP sync, before any cert-based auth.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# clock timezone GMT 0 0

Security & AAA.desc: Daylight Saving Time rule. Common UK: `BST recurring last Sun Mar 1:00 last Sun Oct 2:00`.
Security & AAA.example: WLC-01(config)# clock summer-time BST recurring last Sun Mar 1:00 last Sun Oct 2:00

Security & AAA.desc: Define an NTP source. Configure at least two for redundancy.
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# ntp server 10.10.1.1 prefer
WLC-01(config)# ntp server 10.10.1.2
WLC-01# show ntp associations | include sys.peer|candidate
*~10.10.1.1   sys.peer
+~10.10.1.2   candidate

Security & AAA.desc: Sets regulatory domain. Affects allowed channels and TX power limits. REQUIRED for 6 GHz operation.
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# wireless country GB
WLC-01# show wireless country configured | include GB
GB - United Kingdom : 802.11a Indoor / 802.11b Indoor / 802.11g Indoor

Security & AAA.desc: Globally enable a band that was previously admin-disabled (common culprit when 6 GHz radios are down at first deployment).
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# no ap dot11 6ghz shutdown
WLC-01# show ap dot11 6ghz network | include Status
6 GHz Radio Network Status................................ Enabled

Security & AAA.desc: WLC-internal DHCP scope. For the auth-failed VLAN, omit default-router and dns-server so devices get an IP but cannot reach anything.
Security & AAA.example:
WLC-01(config)# ip dhcp excluded-address 10.6.8.1
WLC-01(config)# ip dhcp excluded-address 10.6.8.2 10.6.8.9
WLC-01(config)# ip dhcp pool AUTH-FAILED-POOL
WLC-01(dhcp-config)# network 10.6.8.0 255.255.255.0
WLC-01(dhcp-config)# lease 0 0 10

mDNS & AP Mesh.desc: Enable mDNS-SD (Service Discovery) gateway for cross-VLAN AirPlay / Bonjour / Chromecast.
mDNS & AP Mesh.example:
WLC-01(config)# mdns-sd gateway
WLC-01(config-mdns-sd)# service-list IN MDNS-IN-LIST IN
WLC-01(config-mdns-sd)# service-list OUT MDNS-OUT-LIST OUT

mDNS & AP Mesh.desc: mDNS service-policy direction inbound (control which services are discovered).
Displays mDNS gateway status and number of learned services
mDNS & AP Mesh.example:
WLC-01# show mdns-sd summary
mDNS Gateway State                    : Enabled
mDNS Service Policy                    : MDNS-POLICY
mDNS AP service policy                 : None
Number of mDNS services discovered     : 23
Number of mDNS clients                : 145

mDNS & AP Mesh.desc: Shows all services discovered and cached by the mDNS gateway
mDNS & AP Mesh.example:
WLC-01# show mdns-sd service-list
Service List Name : MDNS-IN-LIST
Service List Type : IN
Services:
  _airplay._tcp
  _ipp._tcp
  _googlecast._tcp
  _printer._tcp

mDNS & AP Mesh.desc: Displays mesh neighbour relationships for all mesh-capable APs
mDNS & AP Mesh.example:
WLC-01# show ap mesh neighbors
Mesh Neighbors for AP-OUTDOOR-01:
Neighbour MAC      SNR  RAP/MAP  Status
aabb.cc99.0001     45   RAP      LINKED
aabb.cc99.0002     35   MAP      LINKED
aabb.cc99.0003     25   MAP      LINK_INIT

mDNS & AP Mesh.desc: Shows the active mesh tree and path metrics for all mesh APs
mDNS & AP Mesh.example:
WLC-01# show ap mesh path AP-MESH-MAP-01
Mesh Path for AP-MESH-MAP-01:
Hop  AP Name              SNR  Cost
1    AP-MESH-RAP-01       45   1
Total Hops: 1

mDNS & AP Mesh.cmd: ap name <ap-name> mesh backhaul rate auto
mDNS & AP Mesh.desc: Sets the mesh backhaul data rate to automatic on a specific AP
mDNS & AP Mesh.example: WLC-01# ap name AP-MESH-MAP-01 mesh backhaul rate auto

mDNS & AP Mesh.desc: Displays mesh link statistics: SNR, packet counts, and backhaul rates
mDNS & AP Mesh.example:
WLC-01# show wireless mesh stats AP-MESH-MAP-01
Mesh Stats for AP-MESH-MAP-01:
  Backhaul link RX packets: 234567
  Backhaul link TX packets: 198765
  Mesh link errors        : 0
  Mesh path changes       : 2

Diagnostics.desc: Per-client debug capture (modern replacement for `debug client mac-address`). Output goes to a per-MAC log file.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# debug wireless mac aabb.ccdd.eeff
WLC-01# show logging profile wireless filter mac aabb.ccdd.eeff to-file bootflash:client-debug.txt
# Disable when done:
WLC-01# no debug wireless mac aabb.ccdd.eeff

Diagnostics.desc: Disables per-client debugging for a specific MAC address
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# no debug wireless mac aabb.ccdd.eeff
# View captured logs:
WLC-01# show logging profile wireless filter mac aabb.ccdd.eeff to-file bootflash:client-debug.txt

Diagnostics.desc: Displays the contents of the syslog buffer
Diagnostics.example:
Syslog logging: enabled (0 messages dropped, 0 messages rate-limited)
                Console logging: level debugging, 28 messages logged
                Monitor logging: level debugging, 0 messages logged
                Buffer logging: level informational, 421 messages logged
                Trap logging: level informational, 142 message lines logged
                  Logging to 10.50.50.50 (transport tcp port 1514)

Log Buffer (65536 bytes):
*Feb  5 10:18:42.281: %LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN: Line protocol on Interface GigabitEthernet0/1, changed state to up
*Feb  5 10:18:42.291: %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/1, changed state to up
*Feb  5 10:23:01.412: %SYS-5-CONFIG_I: Configured from console by admin on vty0

Diagnostics.desc: ICMP ping from the WLC management interface
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# ping 10.10.10.10
Type escape sequence to abort.
Sending 5, 100-byte ICMP Echos to 10.10.10.10, timeout is 2 seconds:
!!!!!
Success rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 1/2/3 ms

Diagnostics.desc: Traces the network path from the WLC to a destination IP
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# traceroute 10.10.10.10
Type escape sequence to abort.
Tracing the route to 10.10.10.10
  1 10.10.0.254  1 msec  1 msec  1 msec
  2 10.10.10.10  2 msec  2 msec  2 msec

Diagnostics.desc: CDP neighbours seen by APs (typically the switch port the AP is connected to).
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# show ap cdp neighbors
AP Name           AP MAC         Local Port  Neighbour Name        Neighbour Port  Neighbour Platform
AP-OFFICE-01      cc6e.2aae.dc20 GigabitEth0  SWITCH-01             Gi1/0/12        WS-C9300-48P

Diagnostics.desc: Shows LLDP neighbour information as reported by joined APs
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# show ap lldp neighbors
AP Name           AP MAC         Local Port  Neighbour Name  Neighbour Port    Neighbour Platform
AP-OFFICE-01      cc6e.2aae.dc20 Gi0          SWITCH-01       Gi1/0/12          WS-C9300-48P

Diagnostics.desc: Disable all active debugs. Use when you have multiple debugs on and want to clear them all.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# undebug all
All possible debugging has been turned off

Diagnostics.desc: AP image status per AP — current image and any pre-downloaded image.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# show ap image
Total number of APs: 48
Number of APs:
  Currently in same image as Primary  : 48
  Currently in pre-downloaded image   : 0
  Currently downloading               : 0

Diagnostics.desc: Stage a new IOS-XE image to all APs without activating it. Reduces upgrade window.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# ap image predownload
AP image predownload initiated for 48 APs.
# Monitor:
WLC-01# show ap image | include Predownload

Diagnostics.desc: Swap backup ↔ active image on all APs and reboot. Used after `ap image predownload` to apply the staged image.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# ap image swap
AP image swap initiated for 48 APs.
# Monitor progress:
WLC-01# show ap image | include Status

Diagnostics.cmd: show wireless stats ap join detail ap-name <name>
Diagnostics.desc: Detailed join statistics for a specific AP — useful for troubleshooting CAPWAP failures
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# show wireless stats ap join detail ap-name AP-OFFICE-01
AP-OFFICE-01 (cc6e.2aae.dc20)
  Discovery Requests Received: 2
  Join Requests Received: 14
  Successful Joins: 13
  Failed Joins: 1
  DTLS Failures: 1

Diagnostics.desc: CAPWAP Radio Control Block info — radio-level operational state as the AP sees it (run on AP, not WLC).
Diagnostics.example:
AP-OFFICE-01# show capwap client rcb
Slot ID            : 1
Radio Type         : 802.11a/n/ac/ax
Admin State        : ENABLED
Operation State    : UP
Channel            : 149,153 (Auto)
Channel Width      : 40 MHz
TX Power           : 14 dBm (Level 3)
Mode               : REAP
Number of Clients  : 6
Number of BSSIDs   : 2 (CORPORATE-WIFI, GUEST-WIFI)

Diagnostics.desc: Synthetic RADIUS authentication through a server group. Confirms WLC-to-NPS reachability and shared secret. "User rejected" is a positive result (path works); timeout = reachability/secret issue.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# test aaa group RAD-GROUP testuser testpass new-code
User rejected
(A "User rejected" response still confirms WLC-to-NPS RADIUS reachability and
a matching shared secret -- the WLC reached NPS and got a valid RADIUS reply.
A timeout, by contrast, indicates a reachability or shared-secret problem.)

Diagnostics.desc: Capture all RADIUS request/response traffic. Watch for Tunnel-Private-Group-ID in Access-Accept to confirm VLAN assignment. High CPU — disable as soon as you have what you need.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# debug radius
WLC-01# show logging | include RADIUS
%RADIUS: Received from id 1234 10.10.10.10:1812, Access-Accept, len 123
  attr Tunnel-Type 13 [VLAN]
  attr Tunnel-Medium-Type 6 [802]
  attr Tunnel-Private-Group-Id "1110"   ; VLAN assignment
WLC-01# no debug radius

Diagnostics.desc: Detailed per-client 802.1X / association debug. Combine with "show logging | include <mac>" to extract the trace. High CPU — disable when done.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# debug client mac-address aabb.ccdd.eeff
WLC-01# show logging | include aabb.ccdd.eeff
WLC-01# no debug client mac-address aabb.ccdd.eeff

Diagnostics.desc: Trigger HA failover from active to standby. Used to test that clients survive failover with no service impact.
Diagnostics.example:
WLC-01# redundancy force-switchover
This will reload the active unit and force a switchover. Are you sure? [no]: yes
# After failover (~5-10 sec), former standby is now active:
WLC-01# show redundancy | include state
               Current Software state = ACTIVE

Config Management.desc: Saves the running config to NVRAM — persists across reboots
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# copy running-config startup-config
Destination filename [startup-config]?
Building configuration...
[OK]

Config Management.desc: Same as `copy running-config startup-config`. Older muscle-memory.
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# write memory
Building configuration...
[OK]

Config Management.desc: Erase startup-config — DESTRUCTIVE. Device factory-defaults on next reload.
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# write erase
Erasing the nvram filesystem will remove all configuration files! Continue? [confirm] y
[OK]
Erase of nvram: complete

Config Management.desc: Reboot the WLC. HA pair: do `redundancy force-switchover` first to fail-over without service impact.
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# reload
Reload command is being issued on Active unit, this will reload the whole stack
Proceed with reload? [confirm] y

Config Management.desc: Schedule a reload in N minutes. Cancel before it fires with `reload cancel`. Safety net for risky changes over SSH.
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# reload in 5
Reload scheduled in 5 minutes by admin on vty0 (10.99.99.50)
Proceed with reload? [confirm] y

Config Management.desc: Cancel a scheduled reload before it fires. Use as the safety net after a risky change applied over SSH — schedule reload, verify the change works, cancel.
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# reload in 5
Reload scheduled in 5 minutes by admin on vty0 (10.99.99.50)
Proceed with reload? [confirm] y
# Make changes; if they work:
WLC-01# reload cancel
Reload cancelled.
*** --- SHUTDOWN ABORTED ---

Config Management.desc: Backup running-config to TFTP. Prefer SCP/SFTP for untrusted networks.
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# copy running-config tftp:
Address or name of remote host []? 10.50.50.50
Destination filename [WLC-01-confg]? wlc-2026-04-21.cfg
!!
12345 bytes copied in 1.234 secs

Config Management.desc: Restore config from TFTP. Merges with running-config; replaces all.
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# copy tftp: running-config
Address or name of remote host []? 10.50.50.50
Source filename []? wlc-2026-04-21.cfg
[OK - 12345 bytes]

Config Management.desc: IOS-XE in-service upgrade — stage step. WLC stages and pushes new AP images automatically (default 15 APs at a time).
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# software install add file bootflash:/C9800-CL-universalk9.17.12.04.SPA.bin
install_add: START Tue Apr 21 14:30:00 GMT 2026
install_add: Adding IMG
[1] Add succeeded with reason: REASON_NEW_PACKAGE
[2] Add succeeded with reason: REASON_NEW_PACKAGE
SUCCESS: install_add

Config Management.desc: IOS-XE image upgrade — activate step (reload).
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# software install activate
install_activate: START Tue Apr 21 14:35:00 GMT 2026
The system needs to reboot to activate this software. Continue? [y/n]y

Config Management.desc: IOS-XE upgrade — commit step (makes activation persistent across reloads).
Config Management.example:
WLC-01# software install commit
install_commit: START Tue Apr 21 14:45:00 GMT 2026
SUCCESS: install_commit

# AP-FLOOR1-03 at max power — check for coverage hole or excessive path loss

RF.desc: Shows TPC-assigned Tx power for each 6 GHz radio — 6 GHz APs typically run at higher power due to lower baseline interference levels in the new band.

AP CLI — Show & Status.desc: Discover available capwap subcommands. Same trick works for `show dot11 ?`, `show flexconnect ?`, `show controllers ?`, `show wired ?`.
AP CLI — Show & Status.example:
AP-OFFICE-01# show capwap ?
AP-OFFICE-01# show dot11 ?
AP-OFFICE-01# show flexconnect ?
AP-OFFICE-01# show controllers ?
AP-OFFICE-01# show wired ?

AP CLI — Show & Status.desc: AP software version, hardware, uptime. Run on the AP itself, not the WLC.
AP CLI — Show & Status.example:
AP-OFFICE-01# show version
Cisco AP Software, [Cupertino] Catalyst 9100 Series Software, Version 17.12.04, RELEASE SOFTWARE (fc1)

Last reload reason: power-on

cisco CW9166I-ROW (ARMv8 Processor) with 1024MB of memory.
Processor board ID KWC2306G0F1
3 Wireless interfaces (slots 0, 1, 2)
Base Ethernet MAC Address       : 70:bd:96:85:9c:80

AP CLI — Show & Status.desc: AP hardware model and serial number. Used to confirm the right hardware variant (e.g. -E for EU/UK, -A for NA).
AP CLI — Show & Status.example:
AP-OFFICE-01# show inventory

AP CLI — Show & Status.desc: AP system time. CAPWAP join can fail if AP time is far off from the WLC.
AP CLI — Show & Status.example:
AP-OFFICE-01# show clock
*14:32:07.418 GMT Tue Apr 21 2026
# Source is WLC (CAPWAP-syncd):
AP-OFFICE-01# show capwap client config | include Clock
Clock                : Synced with controller

AP CLI — Show & Status.desc: AP local running-config (small — most operational config is pushed via CAPWAP and lives in `show capwap client config`).
AP CLI — Show & Status.example:
AP-OFFICE-01# show running-config
Building configuration...

Current configuration : 2345 bytes

!

!

!
capwap ap primary-base WLC-01 10.10.0.10
capwap ap secondary-base WLC-02 10.10.0.11
!
end

Number of Slots                        : 3

Policy Tag                            : POLICY-FL1
RF Tag                                : RF-DEFAULT
Maintenance Window                    : None

AP CLI — CAPWAP.example:
AP-OFFICE-01# show capwap client rcb

Admin State        : ENABLED

AP CLI — CAPWAP.example:
AP-OFFICE-01# show capwap reap association

1        CORP-WLAN      6              0
